service_line_config.name: angular
service_line_config.tech_context: The frontend is built with Angular. Key considerations: - Framework: Angular 17+ with standalone components, signals, and control flow - Architecture: Feature modules or standalone component architecture, lazy loading - State management: NgRx, Angular Signals, RxJS-based services - Routing: Angular Router with guards, resolvers, lazy-loaded routes - HTTP: HttpClient with interceptors, typed API services - Forms: Reactive Forms (FormBuilder, FormGroup, validators) - UI: Angular Material, PrimeNG, or custom design system with SCSS - Testing: Jasmine + Karma, Jest, Angular Testing Library, Cypress for E2E - Build: Angular CLI, Nx for monorepos, esbuild - DevOps: ng build, Docker, environment.ts configuration

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to Angular feature modules or standalone component groups. Each module should correspond to a lazy-loaded route or feature area in the application.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Reference Angular components, services, and routing when relevant - For form requirements, specify Reactive Forms with validation rules - For data display requirements, specify component hierarchy and data binding - For state requirements, specify NgRx store/effects or signal-based approach
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - Initial bundle size targets and lazy loading strategy - Core Web Vitals: LCP, FID, CLS thresholds - Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance - Browser support matrix - Lighthouse performance score targets - Internationalization (i18n) requirements if applicable
artifact_overrides.task: - Project scaffolding: Angular CLI setup, module structure, shared libraries - Shared components: design system, reusable UI components, SCSS theming - Feature development: components, services, routing, guards, resolvers - State management: store setup, actions, reducers, effects, selectors - Form development: reactive forms, custom validators, error display - API integration: typed HTTP services, interceptors, error handling - Testing: component tests, service tests, E2E tests - Build optimization: lazy loading, tree shaking, bundle analysis
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Test components with Angular Testing Library or TestBed - Test services with HttpClientTestingModule for mocked HTTP - Test NgRx store with mock store providers - Test form validation with various input scenarios - Test routing with RouterTestingModule and guard logic - E2E tests for critical user flows with Cypress
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Application structure: module/standalone organization, lazy-loaded routes - Component architecture: smart vs presentational components, input/output contracts - State management: store structure, action/effect patterns, selector composition - API layer: typed service classes, interceptor chain, error handling strategy - Routing architecture: route hierarchy, guards, resolvers, preloading strategy - Styling architecture: SCSS structure, theming, Angular Material customization - Build architecture: environments, optimization, deployment artifacts
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Angular major version migration effort - Bundle size growth affecting load performance - RxJS complexity and memory leaks from unsubscribed observables - Component coupling making testing and refactoring difficult - Third-party UI library version conflicts with Angular versions - State management complexity in large applications
